content: Just as the human learning framework in high school language arts curricula frames 'reflexion' as a graded, iterative step to reinforce writing skill acquisition, existing LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to incorporate structured, scaffolded reflexion exercises aligned with how humans systematically build self-critical abilities. This approach treats LLM self-reflection not as a one-off post-generation correction step, but as a sequential, graded practice that accumulates better reasoning over time, just like student writing improves through repeated guided reflexion assignments.
